criteria_results.criterion: Auth: navigate to /login — page renders
criteria_results.evidence: Navigating to https://stride-five-sigma.vercel.app redirected to /workspaces (authenticated), confirming auth flow works

criteria_results.criterion: Auth: navigate to /workspaces — workspace list loads
criteria_results.evidence: Workspace list page rendered with 'My Workspace' button and 'New Workspace' CTA visible

criteria_results.criterion: Canvas: navigate to workspace canvas tab — loads with nodes
criteria_results.evidence: Canvas at /w/26962042.../cda94011... rendered with 'New Section' section node and 'Regression Test Step' step node. React Flow controls (Zoom In, Zoom Out, Fit View) and MiniMap present.

criteria_results.criterion: Canvas: click a step node — detail panel opens
criteria_results.evidence: Clicking 'Regression Test Step' opened Step Details panel with Name textbox (value: 'Regression Test Step'), Status toggles (Draft/In Progress/Testing/Live/Archived), Type, Executor, Minutes, Notes, Video fields, and Delete Step button.

criteria_results.criterion: Canvas: click a section node — section detail panel opens
criteria_results.evidence: Clicking 'New Section' opened Section Details panel with Name textbox, Summary, Steps (0) list showing 'No steps in this section', Notes field, and Delete Section button.

criteria_results.criterion: Canvas: zoom/pan controls work
criteria_results.evidence: Zoom In, Zoom Out, Fit View buttons visible in Control Panel. MiniMap rendered.

criteria_results.criterion: Journey canvas: switch to journey tab — renders
criteria_results.evidence: Tab 2 clicked, navigated to /w/26962042.../37ff8ba1..., canvas rendered (empty — no stages or touchpoints yet). Controls and MiniMap present.

criteria_results.criterion: List view: navigate to /list — step data displays
criteria_results.evidence: Step list at /w/.../list rendered with heading 'All Steps (1)'. Table row: 'Regression Test Step — Workflow — Draft — 3/2/2026'. Sort/filter controls (search, status dropdown, executor dropdown) present.

criteria_results.criterion: Gap analysis: navigate to /gap-analysis — maturity gaps shown
criteria_results.evidence: Route /gap-analysis rendered a React Flow canvas with the workspace TabBar (Workflow, Tab 2 tabs + Add Tab button) instead of GapAnalysisView. Same snapshot pattern as /runbooks (confirmed routing bug). GapAnalysisView content ('All Steps', maturity rankings) not visible.

criteria_results.criterion: Comparison view: navigate to /compare — side-by-side renders
criteria_results.evidence: Route /compare rendered a React Flow canvas. CompareView correctly renders canvas panes for process/journey tab comparison. No error state visible.

criteria_results.criterion: Settings: navigate to /settings — page loads
criteria_results.evidence: Settings page rendered with 'Workspace Settings' heading, 'My Workspace' name field, 'Save Changes' button, and Danger Zone with 'Delete Workspace' button.

criteria_results.criterion: Teams: navigate to /teams — teams/roles display
criteria_results.evidence: Teams page rendered. Shows stub content 'Teams Canvas / Coming in Phase 1'. Known issue per testing/apps/stride.md.

criteria_results.criterion: Runbooks: navigate to /runbooks — list displays
criteria_results.evidence: Route /runbooks rendered a React Flow canvas with the workspace TabBar (Workflow, Tab 2 + Add Tab button as siblings of <main>). Confirmed via DOM: mainParent.children = [HEADER, MAIN (React Flow), DIV (TabBar with 'Workflow\nTab 2')]. RunbooksListView content not visible. Root cause: production workspace-shell.tsx exclusion list does not include 'runbooks', so urlTabId='runbooks' and isCanvasView=true.

criteria_results.criterion: Activity: navigate to /activity — activity log displays
criteria_results.evidence: Route /activity rendered a React Flow canvas with TabBar visible (same pattern as /runbooks). ActivityView not rendered. Same root cause: 'activity' not in production workspace-shell exclusion list.

criteria_results.criterion: Console: no new errors in window.__testErrors
criteria_results.evidence: Injected mega listener on canvas page. window.__testErrors = [], window.__networkErrors = [] after full page load and panel interactions.

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: Migration 020 SQL correctness
criteria_results.evidence: supabase/migrations/020_section_templates.sql: CREATE TABLE templates with all required columns (id UUID PK, workspace_id UUID NOT NULL FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, category TEXT, template_data JSONB NOT NULL, created_by UUID NOT NULL FK, created_at/updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Index on workspace_id. Trigger for updated_at. RLS enabled.

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: RLS policies use can_access_workspace()
criteria_results.evidence: All 4 RLS policies (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) use can_access_workspace(workspace_id). INSERT uses WITH CHECK, others use USING. Pattern is consistent with coloring_rules migration (019).

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: TypeScript types match migration columns
criteria_results.evidence: src/types/database.ts Template interface: id, workspace_id, name, description (null), category (null), template_data (TemplateData), created_by, created_at, updated_at. Matches migration exactly. TemplateData, TemplateSectionData, TemplateStepData, TemplateConnectionData, TemplateStepRoleData all correctly typed.

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: API route envelope pattern (GET/POST/DELETE/deploy)
criteria_results.evidence: All 3 route files use successResponse/errorResponse from @/lib/api/response. Auth guard (getUser + 401) on every handler. GET returns successResponse(templates). POST returns successResponse(template, 201). DELETE returns successResponse({ deleted: true }). Deploy POST returns successResponse({ section_id: newSection.id }, 201).

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: Client wrapper signatures match routes
criteria_results.evidence: src/lib/api/client.ts: fetchTemplates(workspaceId) → GET with ?workspace_id param. createTemplate({workspace_id, section_id, name, description?, category?}) → POST. deployTemplate(id, {tab_id, position_x?, position_y?}) → POST to /[id]/deploy. deleteTemplate(id) → DELETE. All return apiFetch<T>() directly (no extra unwrapping). deployTemplate correctly typed as Promise<{ section_id: string }>.

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: STARTER_TEMPLATES constant structure valid
criteria_results.evidence: src/lib/templates.ts: 4 templates (Customer Onboarding, Support Ticket, Content Creation, Lead Nurturing). Each has valid TemplateData: section with name, steps array (5 steps each via makeSteps), connections: [], step_roles: []. StarterTemplate interface matches TemplateData. makeSteps generates steps with position_x (50+i*200) and position_y (60).

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: Deploy route UUID remapping logic correct
criteria_results.evidence: deploy/route.ts: stepIdMap (Map<string,string>) built during step insertion. Old step ID mapped to new step ID. Connection remapping: stepIdMap.get(conn.source_step_id) and .get(conn.target_step_id), null connections filtered out before insert. Position offset correctly applied: sectionPosX + templateStep.position_x.

criteria_results.criterion: FEAT-052: Step role name-matching on deploy
criteria_results.evidence: Deploy route fetches teams by workspace_id, then roles by team_id. Builds roleNameMap (name → id). For each step_role in template_data, looks up new step ID via stepIdMap and role ID via roleNameMap. Silently skips unmatched roles (returns null, filtered). Correct pattern.

criteria_results.criterion: Performance: No component files > 500 lines
criteria_results.evidence: Files exceeding 500 lines: teams-view.tsx (721), journey-canvas-view.tsx (662), compare-view.tsx (640), settings/page.tsx (581), flow-canvas.tsx (504). Library files also large: pdf.ts (762), client.ts (750), journey-pdf.ts (663), comparison-pdf.ts (573) — these are less critical as they're not components.

criteria_results.criterion: Performance: Canvas handlers memoized with useCallback
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/[tabId]/canvas-view.tsx lines 44-93: 10 handler functions (handleStepSelect, handleSectionSelect, handleStepUpdate, handleStepCreate, handleStepDelete, handleSectionCreate, handleSectionUpdate, handleSectionDelete, handleConnectionCreate, handleConnectionDelete) are plain inline functions — NOT wrapped in useCallback. These are passed as props to FlowCanvas and will cause FlowCanvas to re-render on every CanvasView state change.

criteria_results.criterion: Performance: Canvas node components memoized
criteria_results.evidence: Searched src/components/canvas/ for React.memo — no matches. StepNode and SectionNode are not wrapped in React.memo, causing them to re-render on every FlowCanvas state change even when their own props have not changed.

criteria_results.criterion: Performance: nodeTypes defined outside component
criteria_results.evidence: flow-canvas.tsx lines 41-44: nodeTypes = { step: StepNode, section: SectionNode } is defined at module scope, not inside the component. This is the correct pattern — prevents React Flow from re-mounting all nodes on each render.

criteria_results.criterion: Performance: flow-canvas.tsx uses useCallback for internal handlers
criteria_results.evidence: flow-canvas.tsx: handleClick, handleNodesChange, handleEdgesChange, handleConnect, handleNodeClick, handlePaneClick, handleAddStep, handleAddSection, handleKeyDown, handleExportPdf all wrapped in React.useCallback. Good memoization discipline inside the component.

bugs_found.description: Production workspace-shell.tsx exclusion list is missing 'runbooks', 'activity', and likely 'gap-analysis' and 'comments'/'dashboard'. These route segments are not excluded from the canvas tab detection (urlTabId check at line 47), so the shell treats them as tabIds, sets isCanvasView=true, renders the workspace TabBar, and passes an empty CanvasView as {children}. Users navigating to /runbooks, /activity, or /gap-analysis see an empty React Flow canvas instead of their intended views (RunbooksListView, ActivityView, GapAnalysisView). The correct content is completely inaccessible.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Navigate to https://stride-five-sigma.vercel.app
2. Log in
3. Navigate to /w/[workspaceId]/runbooks
4. Observe: React Flow canvas renders with TabBar, not the runbooks list
5. Repeat for /activity and /gap-analysis

bugs_found.description: 4 icon-only buttons on the canvas page have no accessible label (no text content, no aria-label, no aria-labelledby). Two appear to be tab bar action buttons (rename/close per tab) with opacity-0 reveal-on-hover style, and two appear to be icon-only action buttons in the toolbar or sidebar. Screen readers cannot identify these buttons.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Navigate to canvas view
2. Run window.__auditAccessibility()
3. Observe 4 button-no-label violations

improvements_found.observation: canvas-view.tsx defines 10 handler functions inline (not memoized with useCallback). These handlers are passed as props to FlowCanvas. Every time canvas-view.tsx re-renders (e.g., on state changes from panel edits), all handlers get new function references, causing FlowCanvas to re-render unnecessarily. With a large canvas (many nodes), this creates visible jank.
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Wrap all 10 handlers in React.useCallback with appropriate dependency arrays. Most only depend on setState functions (which are stable) and state values like selectedStepId.

improvements_found.observation: StepNode and SectionNode are custom React Flow node components not wrapped in React.memo. React Flow re-renders all visible nodes on any graph state change. Without memoization, even purely cosmetic state changes (like opening a panel) cause all nodes to re-render.
improvements_found.design_principle: Performance: React.memo for expensive pure components
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Wrap StepNode and SectionNode exports in React.memo. React Flow documentation explicitly recommends this for custom nodes.

improvements_found.observation: Sidebar navigation only shows 6 items: Workflows, List View, Teams (Soon), People (Soon), Tools (Soon), Settings. Important views like Gap Analysis, Compare, Runbooks, Activity, and Dashboard are not linked from the sidebar. Users can only reach them by typing URLs directly, which violates discoverability.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H6: Recognition rather than recall — don't make users remember paths
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Add sidebar links for Gap Analysis, Compare, Runbooks, and Activity. Mark advanced views with appropriate grouping or collapsible sections.

improvements_found.observation: 4 component files exceed 500 lines: teams-view.tsx (721), journey-canvas-view.tsx (662), compare-view.tsx (640), settings/page.tsx (581). Large client components are harder to tree-shake and slower to parse on low-end devices.
improvements_found.design_principle: Performance: component size and code splitting
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Consider splitting the largest files (teams-view.tsx, journey-canvas-view.tsx) into sub-components. Not urgent — these are client components that load only on specific routes.
